summary: First-finish smoke test for Apex Media after compile-error bug fix (creation of Contact.jsx, Footer.jsx, ApexChat.jsx). Site compiles, loads end-to-end. All backend endpoints (/api/, POST/GET /api/leads, POST /api/chat/stream w/ X-Session-Id) return 200 with valid payloads. Frontend renders Hero headline 'AI Powered Digital Growth Partner', all sections (services/portfolio/process/clients/contact/footer) and service cards. Apex chat opens, sends, and streams a multi-line assistant reply. Contact form submits successfully with a valid email and renders contact-success.
passed: POST /api/leads -> 200 returns lead object with id
POST /api/chat/stream -> 200 text/plain streaming, X-Session-Id header present, non-empty body
Webpack compile OK; no missing-module or React render errors in console
Hero CTAs (hero-cta-primary, hero-cta-secondary) visible after cinematic loader
Sections visible: services-section, portfolio-section, process-section, clients-section, contact-section, footer
Service cards visible: web-design, seo, ai-chat
Chat launcher opens chat-panel; chat-send streams assistant reply rendered in panel
Contact form (name/email/message) with valid email submits and contact-success renders

failed.issue: POST /api/leads returns 422 when email uses reserved/non-standard TLD like '@apex.test' due to EmailStr (email-validator) strict validation. UI shows contact-error.
failed.evidence: Console: 'Failed to load resource: status 422' when submitting smoke@apex.test; same form passes with smoke@example.com.

action_items: Optional: consider relaxing EmailStr or showing a clearer client-side email validation hint; not a blocker for first finish.
Minor: console warnings from THREE (Clock deprecated) and WebGL GPU stall — cosmetic, not blocking.
